message:
fix(config): reopen restored storage after restore_pending_recovery_backup

`OpenStorageResult::restore_pending_recovery_backup` moved the
fresh-DB aside and copied the original database family back into
place, then left `self.storage = SqliteStorage::open_memory()?` — a
throwaway in-memory handle. The caller was supposed to re-open the
restored file itself, but there was no seam to do so: once
restore_pending_recovery_backup returned, the OpenStorageResult
carried an in-memory storage handle pointing at nothing, and the
next read through `storage.*` saw an empty DB.

This manifested in the failed-import path added by commit 2e8a3ce:
the explicit import failed, restore ran successfully, the sync
command returned the original import error — but subsequent
commands in the same process (or anything that re-used the
OpenStorageResult) saw an empty DB because the restored file on
disk was not actually opened.

Fix: after `restore_database_family_after_failed_rebuild` succeeds,
re-open the file at `self.paths.db_path` with the resolved lock
timeout, wrap any reopen failure in a `BeadsError::WithContext`
that names the restored path, and also reset `self.loaded_jsonl_
hash = None` + `self.auto_rebuilt = false` so a retry against the
OpenStorageResult does not short-circuit thinking the DB was
already rebuilt.

If the reopen itself fails — the disk files are fine but the
process cannot reopen them, e.g. because the lock is held
elsewhere — the error surfaces the exact path that needs attention
in its `context` so the operator sees "restored at '/path/to/db'
but failed to reopen" instead of the opaque "database is locked".
